channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Exxon Mobil CEO expects higher oil prices due to Iran war: ‘The market hasn’t seen the full impact’
item.description: Oil prices have soared on the risk of escalation and then plunged on hopes for peace before repeating the cycle.

item.title: Spirit Airlines prepares to shut down after failing to reach deal for bailout lifeline
item.description: Talks with bondholders for a government bailout this week failed to yield a deal.

item.title: Cautious optimism: Berkshire investors weigh future under new CEO Greg Abel
item.description: The mood in the air was cautiously optimistic as new and returning investors weighed the company's direction under a new chief executive.

item.title: Trump tells Congress hostilities in Iran 'have terminated' as war powers deadline hits
item.description: Trump said there "is tremendous discord" among Iran's leaders, which has complicated efforts to end the war that the United States began in late February.

item.title: Jobs and earnings will dominate the first full week of May. Here's what's ahead
item.description: A solid jobs report could alleviate fears of a weakening U.S. economy, which some recent signals have indicated.

item.title: Bard College President Leon Botstein retiring after Jeffrey Epstein ties detailed
item.description: Bard College's long-time leader Leon Botstein has faced pressure since the DOJ released documents about his communications with Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway’s shopping extravaganza draws lighter crowds as spotlight shifts to Greg Abel
item.description: Greg Abel made a point of stopping by every booth in the hall, greeting employees and shaking hands with shareholders.

item.title: Spirit Airlines could shut down overnight. Here's what travelers need to know
item.description: What travelers who are ticketed on Spirit Airlines need to know if the carrier shuts down.

item.title: S&P 500 closes at a new record to usher in May as oil prices cool and Apple rises: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 rose to a fresh all-time intraday high on Friday, boosted by Apple shares, while oil prices fell as a new month of trading got underway.

item.title: Trump says he's raising EU auto tariffs to 25%
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down Trump's "reciprocal" tariffs earlier this year, and Europe warned that its trade deal with Washington could be in jeopardy.

item.title: Apollo Sports Capital and Tom Dundon make landmark $225 million investment in pickleball
item.description: The fresh funds bring the total investment in Pickleball Inc. to $315 million, as investors continue to look at emerging sports as a place to park their money.

item.title: Why you won't find Kentucky Derby bets on prediction platforms
item.description: The Kentucky Derby takes place Saturday in Louisville. Prediction market platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket are sitting the race out.

item.title: Jim Cramer says the market powered through a tough earnings week but 'that doesn't mean we're out of the woods yet'
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer looks to the jam-packed earnings week ahead and explains what he's looking for.

item.title: Engine trouble ahead? How the Strait of Hormuz standoff threatens luxury auto giants
item.description: Supercar engines rely on base oils because they can withstand extreme heat, high revs and intense pressure.

item.title: Pirro keeps pressure on Fed's Powell despite dropping probe
item.description: The U.S. attorney is expected to imminently appeal a ruling blocking her investigation of the Fed's cost overruns.

item.title: Apple's stock gains as company execs cite iPhone, Mac demand in boosting guidance
item.description: On its quarterly earnings call, Apple said revenue growth in the current quarter would be between 14% and 17%, well above analysts' estimates.

item.title: Trump's new executive order, with help from Congress, could increase U.S. retirement wealth up to 77%, researchers find
item.description: Trump's executive order created a new way for Americans to save for retirement and called on Congress to expand the parameters of the deal.

item.title: A major shift could soon happen in the Mag 7
item.description: Quietly, shares of Google-parent Alphabet are on the verge of topping $5 trillion, and overtaking AI-leader Nvidia as the world's most valuable company.

item.title: BlackRock says these bonds have attractive yields — and can help insulate from AI disruption
item.description: Investors may want to focus on bonds that are tied to the real economy, BlackRock says.

item.title: Six months out, control of the Senate is a dead heat, traders on Kalshi say
item.description: While Democrats are favored to flip control of the House of Representatives, the upper chamber is a much closer race, traders believe.

item.title: What Sandisk's play on surging memory prices means for our tech stocks
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: No need to wait for the TrumpIRA to start saving for retirement: 3 tools to tap right now
item.description: How IRAs, annuities and mortgage refinancing can help you build retirement savings right now.

item.title: Fed dissenters explain 'no' votes, saying they disagreed with hinting next move would be a cut
item.description: Federal Reserve officials who voted this week against the post-meeting statement said they didn't think it was appropriate to signal that the next interest rate move would be lower.

item.title: Inside the Fed: Powell vows he won't be a 'shadow chair,' but a Warsh clash will be tough to avoid
item.description: When the Fed gathers again, it will mark the first time a sitting and former chair conduct business together in nearly 80 years.

item.title: UK exports to U.S. plunge by 25% after Trump's 'liberation day' tariffs blitz
item.description: The U.K. is now running a trade deficit with its largest trading partner.

item.title: Bitcoin surged in April, but weak buyer demand makes the rally vulnerable
item.description: Bitcoin surged in April, but its run could be on shaky ground, according to CryptoQuant.

item.title: Atlassian stock soars 29% after earnings show strong cloud, data center growth
item.description: Atlassian's stock has been hit hard in the "SaaS-pocalypse" sweeping software names as AI threatens to disrupt their business models.

item.title: Lutnick gets grilling on Nvidia chip sales to China in letter from Sen. Chris Coons
item.description: Sen. Chris Coons demanded answers on H200 chips sales to China after Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and the Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang give differing answers.

item.title: Apple proves once again it is the ultimate cash machine. How to trade the stock from here
item.description: While the rest of the 'Mag 7' is caught up in the artificial intelligence arms race, Apple remains the ultimate shareholder 'cash machine.'

item.title: Roblox shares plummet 18% as child safety measures weigh on bookings
item.description: Roblox is facing over 140 federal lawsuits accusing it of failing to prevent child exploitation, and last month settled with Alabama and West Virginia.
